data.title: Ethereum Futures Turn Bearish as Sell Pressure Builds on Binance
data.description:
Ethereum derivatives positioning has shifted meaningfully over the past month. According to a recent report shared by CryptoQuant, Binance data now reflects a sustained tilt toward aggressive selling. The Ethereum Taker Buy Sell Ratio on Binance, measured using a 30-day simple moving average (SMA 30), has declined to 0. 97, its lowest level since November 2025
source: https://www.ethnews.com/ethereum-futures-turn-bearish-as-sell-pressure-builds-on-binance/

data.title: AI Expansion May Lift US Carbon Emissions by 900,000 Tons
data.description:
A new study estimates AI adoption could add about 900,000 tons of CO2 annually in the US, a small but notable increase tied to rising electricity demand.
source: https://catenaa.com/industries/technology/ai-expansion-may-lift-us-carbon-emissions-by-900000-tons/

data.title: USDC Circulation Increases by 2.6 Billion in One Week
data.description: Circle's website reports that in the week ending February 12, approximately 8.4 billion USDC were issued, while about 5.8 billion were redeemed. According to Odaily, this resulted in a net increase of around 2.6 billion USDC in circulation. As of February 12, the total circulation of USDC stood at approximately 73.1 billion, with reserve assets valued at around 73.4 billion USD.

data.title: SpaceX Approved to Launch Starlink Internet Service in Vietnam
data.description: Vietnam's government has approved SpaceX to launch its Starlink satellite internet service in the country, according to Jin10. On February 14, the Ministry of Science and Technology granted a license to SpaceX's local subsidiary, allowing it to provide both fixed and mobile satellite internet services. The company is also authorized to use radio frequencies and equipment for its operations.

data.title: USDC issuance increased by approximately 2.6 billion coins in one week.
data.description: According to information on Circle's official website, in the week ending February 12th local time, approximately 8.4 billion USDC were issued, approximately 5.8 billion were redeemed, and the circulating supply increased by approximately 2.6 billion. As of February 12th local time, the circulating supply of USDC was approximately 73.1 billion, and the value of its reserve assets was approximately US$73.4 billion.

data.title: Roundhill plans to launch six event-contract ETFs related to the outcome of the 2028 US presidential election.
data.description: Eric Balchunas, a senior ETF analyst at Bloomberg, wrote in an article on X that Roundhill Investments, a U.S. ETF issuer, has filed an application with the SEC to launch six event-contract ETFs related to the outcome of the 2028 U.S. presidential election.

data.title: Truth Social Funds has filed with the SEC to launch an ETF related to Bitcoin, Ethereum, and CROs.
data.description: Truth Social Funds, owned by Trump Media & Technology Group, has filed applications with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) for the Truth Social Cronos Yield Maximizer ETF and the Truth Social Bitcoin and Ether ETF. The former aims to seek investment returns based on the performance of CROs and their associated staking rewards. The latter aims to seek investment returns based on the combined performance of Bitcoin and Ethereum and their associated staking rewards. Crypto.com will act as the digital asset custodian, liquidity provider, and staking service provider for the funds. Both ETFs will be available for purchase through Crypto.com's broker-dealer, Foris Capital US LLC.

data.title: Crypto markets steady before Fed minutes, PCE, tariff case
data.description:
How Fed minutes, core PCE inflation, Supreme Court ruling on Trump tariffs tie policy guidance and tariff risk to market pricing in a short week for traders.
source: https://coincu.com/news/crypto-markets-steady-before-fed-minutes-pce-tariff-case/

data.title: White House Valentine's Day Cards Spark Controversy
data.description: On February 14, Valentine's Day, the White House released a series of cards on the social media platform X, featuring designs 'just for you,' including visual elements like Greenland surrounded by hearts. According to Jin10, this set of cards has sparked controversy, with many netizens criticizing the White House for being 'inappropriate' and questioning the misuse of social media by U.S. government officials. The White House released four cards in total, one of which placed a map of Greenland within a heart shape, accompanied by the caption, 'It's time to give our ambiguous relationship a name.'

data.title: Roundhill’s election event contract ETFs ‘potentially groundbreaking'
data.description:
The ETF issuer warned investors that selecting the fund tied to the losing US presidential outcome could result in the loss of nearly all invested capital.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/roundhill-investments-event-contracts-prediction-markets-etf-united-states-election?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: MultiBank to Unlock 25 Million Tokens in February
data.description: MultiBank (MBG) is set to unlock approximately 25 million tokens on February 22 at 8 a.m. UTC+8. According to ChainCatcher, this token release is part of the scheduled unlocks tracked by the Web3 asset data platform, RootData.

data.title: Polkadot's Hydration Faces Unfollow Trend Among Influential Figures
data.description:
In the past week, Polkadot's liquidity protocol Hydration has seen a significant number of unfollows from prominent figures on X, according to ChainCatcher. Influential individuals such as cryptocurrency trader Loomdart, DeFi analyst Ignas, and angel investor naniXBT have recently unfollowed the project.

Additionally, Saymore has also experienced a notable number of unfollows from top personalities on X.

data.title: US senators have called for an investigation into Trump-related crypto transactions, including a $500 million investment in the UAE that poses national security risks.
data.description: Two Democratic senators have written to Treasury Secretary Scott Bessant, requesting an assessment of whether a $500 million investment by a UAE government-affiliated entity in crypto company World Liberty Financial should be subject to a national security review by the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States (CFIUS). This committee, led by the Treasury Department, reviews foreign investment transactions that may involve sensitive technologies or data. According to a previous report by The Wall Street Journal, G42, backed by Abu Dhabi royal family member Sheikh Tahmorn bin Zayed, acquired a 49% stake in World Liberty Financial through an entity called Aryam Investment 1. The report indicated that some of the funds flowed to the Trump family and its affiliates. The Trump administration denied any knowledge of this. Senators Elizabeth Warren and Andy King questioned in their letter whether the transaction could grant a foreign government access to user data, and cited G42's past collaborations with Chinese companies. They requested the Treasury Department to explain by March 5 whether it had initiated a review process or made recommendations to the President. World Liberty Financial is the issuer of the stablecoin USD1, which has a circulating supply of over $5 billion since its launch in March 2025. The company lists Trump and his Middle East envoy Steve Vitkov as honorary co-founders. Previously, a company spokesperson stated that neither of them was involved in transactions related to the UAE.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Gold Prices Rebound Above $5,000 Amid Rate Cut Speculation
data.description: Gold prices have rebounded above the $5,000 mark following a decline in the core consumer price index in the United States. According to Ming Pao, this development has increased market expectations for potential interest rate cuts. Gold closed at $5,042 per ounce, marking a 2.33% rise for the day and a 1.53% increase over the week. Despite this recovery, gold remains 10.1% below its historical high of $5,608. Meanwhile, silver prices also saw an uptick, closing at $77.41 per ounce with a 2.08% increase on Friday.

data.title: X Platform to Redirect Trades to External Brokers
data.description: Nikita Bier, the product lead for X, announced on the X platform that the company will not handle trade execution or act as a broker, but will instead focus on developing financial data tools and links. According to Odaily, this indicates that transactions initiated on the X platform will be redirected to external brokers or exchange partners for execution. Previously, Bier mentioned that X plans to introduce several features in the coming weeks, including 'Smart Cashtags,' which will allow users to trade stocks and cryptocurrencies directly from their timelines.

data.title: Global Markets Brace for Key Economic Data Releases and Policy Decisions
data.description:
Next week, global markets are set to experience a 'data bombardment,' with several significant economic reports and policy decisions scheduled. According to BlockBeats, the Federal Reserve will release the minutes of its January monetary policy meeting on Thursday at 03:00 UTC+8, providing insights into the potential interest rate cut path for 2026. On Friday at 21:30 UTC+8, the initial U.S. GDP figures for the fourth quarter and the core PCE price index will be announced. As the Fed's preferred inflation measure, a higher-than-expected PCE reading could influence the pace of policy easing this year.

Central banks will also be in focus, with multiple Federal Reserve officials set to speak, the Reserve Bank of Australia releasing its meeting minutes, and the Reserve Bank of New Zealand announcing its rate decision. The market generally anticipates that the New Zealand central bank will maintain its current stance, while the Australian central bank may emphasize inflationary pressures.

In terms of asset performance, spot gold remains volatile at high levels, having rebounded after a significant drop earlier in the week. Oil prices have surged and then retreated, amid speculation that OPEC+ might resume production increases in April. The U.S. dollar's movement is a critical factor; if it does not hit new lows, it may remain relatively strong, especially as tariff rulings approach. Meanwhile, demand for U.S. Treasury bonds is rising, potentially triggering a new wave of asset reallocation.

A major event on the horizon is the Supreme Court of the United States' opinion release on February 20, which includes a ruling on U.S. President Donald Trump's 'Liberation Day' tariff policy. An unfavorable decision could significantly impact these tariff measures, affecting global trade and market sentiment.

On the corporate front, the earnings season is nearing its end. Walmart is set to announce its fourth-quarter fiscal results, with the market closely watching key indicators such as e-commerce growth, AI applications, and tariff impacts. Walmart's guidance could have a substantial effect on retail and overall market sentiment.

data.title: Concerns Over AI Agent Development During Spring Festival
data.description: Crypto KOL Xingpt posted on X about the challenges faced during the Spring Festival, highlighting concerns not just about raising a child, but also about developing an AI agent. Xingpt expressed frustration that their AI agent lacks capabilities compared to others, noting that while some agents can autonomously generate income for their owners, theirs incurs a monthly cost of several dozen dollars. The post humorously suggests that focusing on improving AI agents might be more beneficial than traditional parenting efforts.
